{
  "filePath": "/workspace/flink-runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/flink/streaming/runtime/operators/windowing/EvictingWindowOperator.java",
  "newString": "            for (W window : elementWindows) {\n\n                // adding the new window might result in a merge, in that case the actualWindow\n                // is the merged window and we work with that. If we don't merge then\n                // actualWindow == window\n                W actualWindow;\n                try {\n                    actualWindow =\n                            mergingWindows.addWindow(\n                                    window,\n                                    new MergingWindowSet.MergeFunction<W>() {\n                                        @Override\n                                        public void merge(\n                                                W mergeResult,\n                                                Collection<W> mergedWindows,\n                                                W stateWindowResult,\n                                                Collection<W> mergedStateWindows)\n                                                throws Exception {\n\n                                            if ((windowAssigner.isEventTime()\n                                                    && mergeResult.maxTimestamp() + allowedLateness\n                                                            <= internalTimerService\n                                                                    .currentWatermark())) {\n                                                throw new UnsupportedOperationException(\n                                                        \"The end timestamp of an \"\n                                                                + \"event-time window cannot become earlier than the current watermark \"\n                                                                + \"by merging. Current watermark: \"\n                                                                + internalTimerService\n                                                                        .currentWatermark()\n                                                                + \" window: \"\n                                                                + mergeResult);\n                                            } else if (!windowAssigner.isEventTime()\n                                                    && mergeResult.maxTimestamp()\n                                                            <= internalTimerService\n                                                                    .currentProcessingTime()) {\n                                                throw new UnsupportedOperationException(\n                                                        \"The end timestamp of a \"\n                                                                + \"processing-time window cannot become earlier than the current processing time \"\n                                                                + \"by merging. Current processing time: \"\n                                                                + internalTimerService\n                                                                        .currentProcessingTime()\n                                                                + \" window: \"\n                                                                + mergeResult);\n                                            }\n\n                                            triggerContext.key = key;\n                                            triggerContext.window = mergeResult;\n\n                                            triggerContext.onMerge(mergedWindows);\n\n                                            for (W m : mergedWindows) {\n                                                triggerContext.window = m;\n                                                triggerContext.clear();\n                                                deleteCleanupTimer(m);\n                                            }\n\n                                            // merge the merged state windows into the newly resulting\n                                            // state window\n                                            evictingWindowState.mergeNamespaces(\n                                                    stateWindowResult, mergedStateWindows);\n                                        }\n                                    });\n                } catch (UnsupportedOperationException e) {\n                    // If a late element would result in merging into an already-expired window,\n                    // skip it and treat it as a late element to be side-output later\n                    continue;\n                }\n\n                // drop if the window is already late\n                if (isWindowLate(actualWindow)) {\n                    mergingWindows.retireWindow(actualWindow);\n                    continue;\n                }\n                isSkippedElement = false;",
  "oldString": "            for (W window : elementWindows) {\n\n                // adding the new window might result in a merge, in that case the actualWindow\n                // is the merged window and we work with that. If we don't merge then\n                // actualWindow == window\n                W actualWindow =\n                        mergingWindows.addWindow(\n                                window,\n                                new MergingWindowSet.MergeFunction<W>() {\n                                    @Override\n                                    public void merge(\n                                            W mergeResult,\n                                            Collection<W> mergedWindows,\n                                            W stateWindowResult,\n                                            Collection<W> mergedStateWindows)\n                                            throws Exception {\n\n                                        if ((windowAssigner.isEventTime()\n                                                && mergeResult.maxTimestamp() + allowedLateness\n                                                        <= internalTimerService\n                                                                .currentWatermark())) {\n                                            throw new UnsupportedOperationException(\n                                                    \"The end timestamp of an \"\n                                                            + \"event-time window cannot become earlier than the current watermark \"\n                                                            + \"by merging. Current watermark: \"\n                                                            + internalTimerService\n                                                                    .currentWatermark()\n                                                            + \" window: \"\n                                                            + mergeResult);\n                                        } else if (!windowAssigner.isEventTime()\n                                                && mergeResult.maxTimestamp()\n                                                        <= internalTimerService\n                                                                .currentProcessingTime()) {\n                                            throw new UnsupportedOperationException(\n                                                    \"The end timestamp of a \"\n                                                            + \"processing-time window cannot become earlier than the current processing time \"\n                                                            + \"by merging. Current processing time: \"\n                                                            + internalTimerService\n                                                                    .currentProcessingTime()\n                                                            + \" window: \"\n                                                            + mergeResult);\n                                        }\n\n                                        triggerContext.key = key;\n                                        triggerContext.window = mergeResult;\n\n                                        triggerContext.onMerge(mergedWindows);\n\n                                        for (W m : mergedWindows) {\n                                            triggerContext.window = m;\n                                            triggerContext.clear();\n                                            deleteCleanupTimer(m);\n                                        }\n\n                                        // merge the merged state windows into the newly resulting\n                                        // state window\n                                        evictingWindowState.mergeNamespaces(\n                                                stateWindowResult, mergedStateWindows);\n                                    }\n                                });\n\n                // drop if the window is already late\n                if (isWindowLate(actualWindow)) {\n                    mergingWindows.retireWindow(actualWindow);\n                    continue;\n                }\n                isSkippedElement = false;",
  "originalFile": "/*\n * Licensed to the Apache Software Foundation (ASF) under one\n * or more contributor license agreements.  See the NOTICE file\n * distributed with this work for additional information\n * regarding copyright ownership.  The ASF licenses this file\n * to you under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the\n * \"License\"); you may not use this file except in compliance\n * with the License.  You may obtain a copy of the License at\n *\n * http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n *\n * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n * distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n * limitations under the License.\n */\n\npackage org.apache.flink.streaming.runtime.operators.windowing;\n\nimport org.apache.flink.annotation.Internal;\nimport org.apache.flink.annotation.VisibleForTesting;\nimport org.apache.flink.api.common.state.AppendingState;\nimport org.apache.flink.api.common.state.ListState;\nimport org.apache.flink.api.common.state.StateDescriptor;\nimport org.apache.flink.api.common.typeutils.TypeSerializer;\nimport org.apache.flink.api.java.functions.KeySelector;\nimport org.apache.flink.metrics.MetricGroup;\nimport org.apache.flink.runtime.state.internal.InternalListState;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.api.operators.InternalTimer;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.api.windowing.assigners.MergingWindowAssigner;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.api.windowing.assigners.WindowAssigner;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.api.windowing.evictors.Evictor;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.api.windowing.triggers.Trigger;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.api.windowing.triggers.TriggerResult;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.api.windowing.windows.Window;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.runtime.operators.windowing.functions.InternalWindowFunction;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.runtime.streamrecord.StreamRecord;\nimport org.apache.flink.util.OutputTag;\n\nimport org.apache.flink.shaded.guava33.com.google.common.base.Function;\nimport org.apache.flink.shaded.guava33.com.google.common.collect.FluentIterable;\nimport org.apache.flink.shaded.guava33.com.google.common.collect.Iterables;\n\nimport java.util.Collection;\nimport java.util.stream.Collectors;\n\nimport static org.apache.flink.util.Preconditions.checkNotNull;\n\n/**\n * A {@link WindowOperator} that also allows an {@link Evictor} to be used.\n *\n * <p>The {@code Evictor} is used to remove elements from a pane before/after the evaluation of\n * {@link InternalWindowFunction} and after the window evaluation gets triggered by a {@link\n * org.apache.flink.streaming.api.windowing.triggers.Trigger}.\n *\n * @param <K> The type of key returned by the {@code KeySelector}.\n * @param <IN> The type of the incoming elements.\n * @param <OUT> The type of elements emitted by the {@code InternalWindowFunction}.\n * @param <W> The type of {@code Window} that the {@code WindowAssigner} assigns.\n */\n@Internal\npublic class EvictingWindowOperator<K, IN, OUT, W extends Window>\n        extends WindowOperator<K, IN, Iterable<IN>, OUT, W> {\n\n    private static final long serialVersionUID = 1L;\n\n    // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\n    // these fields are set by the API stream graph builder to configure the operator\n\n    private final Evictor<? super IN, ? super W> evictor;\n\n    private final StateDescriptor<? extends ListState<StreamRecord<IN>>, ?>\n            evictingWindowStateDescriptor;\n\n    // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\n    // the fields below are instantiated once the operator runs in the runtime\n\n    private transient EvictorContext evictorContext;\n\n    private transient InternalListState<K, W, StreamRecord<IN>> evictingWindowState;\n\n    // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    public EvictingWindowOperator(\n            WindowAssigner<? super IN, W> windowAssigner,\n            TypeSerializer<W> windowSerializer,\n            KeySelector<IN, K> keySelector,\n            TypeSerializer<K> keySerializer,\n            StateDescriptor<? extends ListState<StreamRecord<IN>>, ?> windowStateDescriptor,\n            InternalWindowFunction<Iterable<IN>, OUT, K, W> windowFunction,\n            Trigger<? super IN, ? super W> trigger,\n            Evictor<? super IN, ? super W> evictor,\n            long allowedLateness,\n            OutputTag<IN> lateDataOutputTag) {\n\n        super(\n                windowAssigner,\n                windowSerializer,\n                keySelector,\n                keySerializer,\n                null,\n                windowFunction,\n                trigger,\n                allowedLateness,\n                lateDataOutputTag);\n\n        this.evictor = checkNotNull(evictor);\n        this.evictingWindowStateDescriptor = checkNotNull(windowStateDescriptor);\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void processElement(StreamRecord<IN> element) throws Exception {\n        final Collection<W> elementWindows =\n                windowAssigner.assignWindows(\n                        element.getValue(), element.getTimestamp(), windowAssignerContext);\n\n        // if element is handled by none of assigned elementWindows\n        boolean isSkippedElement = true;\n\n        final K key = this.<K>getKeyedStateBackend().getCurrentKey();\n\n        if (windowAssigner instanceof MergingWindowAssigner) {\n            MergingWindowSet<W> mergingWindows = getMergingWindowSet();\n\n            for (W window : elementWindows) {\n\n                // adding the new window might result in a merge, in that case the actualWindow\n                // is the merged window and we work with that. If we don't merge then\n                // actualWindow == window\n                W actualWindow =\n                        mergingWindows.addWindow(\n                                window,\n                                new MergingWindowSet.MergeFunction<W>() {\n                                    @Override\n                                    public void merge(\n                                            W mergeResult,\n                                            Collection<W> mergedWindows,\n                                            W stateWindowResult,\n                                            Collection<W> mergedStateWindows)\n                                            throws Exception {\n\n                                        if ((windowAssigner.isEventTime()\n                                                && mergeResult.maxTimestamp() + allowedLateness\n                                                        <= internalTimerService\n                                                                .currentWatermark())) {\n                                            throw new UnsupportedOperationException(\n                                                    \"The end timestamp of an \"\n                                                            + \"event-time window cannot become earlier than the current watermark \"\n                                                            + \"by merging. Current watermark: \"\n                                                            + internalTimerService\n                                                                    .currentWatermark()\n                                                            + \" window: \"\n                                                            + mergeResult);\n                                        } else if (!windowAssigner.isEventTime()\n                                                && mergeResult.maxTimestamp()\n                                                        <= internalTimerService\n                                                                .currentProcessingTime()) {\n                                            throw new UnsupportedOperationException(\n                                                    \"The end timestamp of a \"\n                                                            + \"processing-time window cannot become earlier than the current processing time \"\n                                                            + \"by merging. Current processing time: \"\n                                                            + internalTimerService\n                                                                    .currentProcessingTime()\n                                                            + \" window: \"\n                                                            + mergeResult);\n                                        }\n\n                                        triggerContext.key = key;\n                                        triggerContext.window = mergeResult;\n\n                                        triggerContext.onMerge(mergedWindows);\n\n                                        for (W m : mergedWindows) {\n                                            triggerContext.window = m;\n                                            triggerContext.clear();\n                                            deleteCleanupTimer(m);\n                                        }\n\n                                        // merge the merged state windows into the newly resulting\n                                        // state window\n                                        evictingWindowState.mergeNamespaces(\n                                                stateWindowResult, mergedStateWindows);\n                                    }\n                                });\n\n                // drop if the window is already late\n                if (isWindowLate(actualWindow)) {\n                    mergingWindows.retireWindow(actualWindow);\n                    continue;\n                }\n                isSkippedElement = false;\n\n                W stateWindow = mergingWindows.getStateWindow(actualWindow);\n                if (stateWindow == null) {\n                    throw new IllegalStateException(\n                            \"Window \" + window + \" is not in in-flight window set.\");\n                }\n\n                evictingWindowState.setCurrentNamespace(stateWindow);\n                evictingWindowState.add(element);\n\n                triggerContext.key = key;\n                triggerContext.window = actualWindow;\n                evictorContext.key = key;\n                evictorContext.window = actualWindow;\n\n                TriggerResult triggerResult = triggerContext.onElement(element);\n\n                if (triggerResult.isFire()) {\n                    Iterable<StreamRecord<IN>> contents = evictingWindowState.get();\n                    if (contents == null) {\n                        // if we have no state, there is nothing to do\n                        continue;\n                    }\n                    emitWindowContents(actualWindow, contents, evictingWindowState);\n                }\n\n                if (triggerResult.isPurge()) {\n                    evictingWindowState.clear();\n                }\n                registerCleanupTimer(actualWindow);\n            }\n\n            // need to make sure to update the merging state in state\n            mergingWindows.persist();\n        } else {\n            for (W window : elementWindows) {\n\n                // check if the window is already inactive\n                if (isWindowLate(window)) {\n                    continue;\n                }\n                isSkippedElement = false;\n\n                evictingWindowState.setCurrentNamespace(window);\n                evictingWindowState.add(element);\n\n                triggerContext.key = key;\n                triggerContext.window = window;\n                evictorContext.key = key;\n                evictorContext.window = window;\n\n                TriggerResult triggerResult = triggerContext.onElement(element);\n\n                if (triggerResult.isFire()) {\n                    Iterable<StreamRecord<IN>> contents = evictingWindowState.get();\n                    if (contents == null) {\n                        // if we have no state, there is nothing to do\n                        continue;\n                    }\n                    emitWindowContents(window, contents, evictingWindowState);\n                }\n\n                if (triggerResult.isPurge()) {\n                    evictingWindowState.clear();\n                }\n                registerCleanupTimer(window);\n            }\n        }\n\n        // side output input event if\n        // element not handled by any window\n        // late arriving tag has been set\n        // windowAssigner is event time and current timestamp + allowed lateness no less than\n        // element timestamp\n        if (isSkippedElement && isElementLate(element)) {\n            if (lateDataOutputTag != null) {\n                sideOutput(element);\n            } else {\n                this.numLateRecordsDropped.inc();\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void onEventTime(InternalTimer<K, W> timer) throws Exception {\n\n        triggerContext.key = timer.getKey();\n        triggerContext.window = timer.getNamespace();\n        evictorContext.key = timer.getKey();\n        evictorContext.window = timer.getNamespace();\n\n        MergingWindowSet<W> mergingWindows = null;\n\n        if (windowAssigner instanceof MergingWindowAssigner) {\n            mergingWindows = getMergingWindowSet();\n            W stateWindow = mergingWindows.getStateWindow(triggerContext.window);\n            if (stateWindow == null) {\n                // Timer firing for non-existent window, this can only happen if a\n                // trigger did not clean up timers. We have already cleared the merging\n                // window and therefore the Trigger state, however, so nothing to do.\n                return;\n            } else {\n                evictingWindowState.setCurrentNamespace(stateWindow);\n            }\n        } else {\n            evictingWindowState.setCurrentNamespace(triggerContext.window);\n        }\n\n        TriggerResult triggerResult = triggerContext.onEventTime(timer.getTimestamp());\n\n        if (triggerResult.isFire()) {\n            Iterable<StreamRecord<IN>> contents = evictingWindowState.get();\n            if (contents != null) {\n                emitWindowContents(triggerContext.window, contents, evictingWindowState);\n            }\n        }\n\n        if (triggerResult.isPurge()) {\n            evictingWindowState.clear();\n        }\n\n        if (windowAssigner.isEventTime()\n                && isCleanupTime(triggerContext.window, timer.getTimestamp())) {\n            clearAllState(triggerContext.window, evictingWindowState, mergingWindows);\n        }\n\n        if (mergingWindows != null) {\n            // need to make sure to update the merging state in state\n            mergingWindows.persist();\n        }\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void onProcessingTime(InternalTimer<K, W> timer) throws Exception {\n        triggerContext.key = timer.getKey();\n        triggerContext.window = timer.getNamespace();\n        evictorContext.key = timer.getKey();\n        evictorContext.window = timer.getNamespace();\n\n        MergingWindowSet<W> mergingWindows = null;\n\n        if (windowAssigner instanceof MergingWindowAssigner) {\n            mergingWindows = getMergingWindowSet();\n            W stateWindow = mergingWindows.getStateWindow(triggerContext.window);\n            if (stateWindow == null) {\n                // Timer firing for non-existent window, this can only happen if a\n                // trigger did not clean up timers. We have already cleared the merging\n                // window and therefore the Trigger state, however, so nothing to do.\n                return;\n            } else {\n                evictingWindowState.setCurrentNamespace(stateWindow);\n            }\n        } else {\n            evictingWindowState.setCurrentNamespace(triggerContext.window);\n        }\n\n        TriggerResult triggerResult = triggerContext.onProcessingTime(timer.getTimestamp());\n\n        if (triggerResult.isFire()) {\n            Iterable<StreamRecord<IN>> contents = evictingWindowState.get();\n            if (contents != null) {\n                emitWindowContents(triggerContext.window, contents, evictingWindowState);\n            }\n        }\n\n        if (triggerResult.isPurge()) {\n            evictingWindowState.clear();\n        }\n\n        if (!windowAssigner.isEventTime()\n                && isCleanupTime(triggerContext.window, timer.getTimestamp())) {\n            clearAllState(triggerContext.window, evictingWindowState, mergingWindows);\n        }\n\n        if (mergingWindows != null) {\n            // need to make sure to update the merging state in state\n            mergingWindows.persist();\n        }\n    }\n\n    private void emitWindowContents(\n            W window, Iterable<StreamRecord<IN>> contents, ListState<StreamRecord<IN>> windowState)\n            throws Exception {\n        timestampedCollector.setAbsoluteTimestamp(window.maxTimestamp());\n\n        // Work around type system restrictions...\n        FluentIterable<TimestampedValue<IN>> recordsWithTimestamp =\n                FluentIterable.from(contents)\n                        .transform(\n                                new Function<StreamRecord<IN>, TimestampedValue<IN>>() {\n                                    @Override\n                                    public TimestampedValue<IN> apply(StreamRecord<IN> input) {\n                                        return TimestampedValue.from(input);\n                                    }\n                                });\n        evictorContext.evictBefore(recordsWithTimestamp, Iterables.size(recordsWithTimestamp));\n\n        FluentIterable<IN> projectedContents =\n                recordsWithTimestamp.transform(\n                        new Function<TimestampedValue<IN>, IN>() {\n                            @Override\n                            public IN apply(TimestampedValue<IN> input) {\n                                return input.getValue();\n                            }\n                        });\n\n        processContext.window = triggerContext.window;\n        userFunction.process(\n                triggerContext.key,\n                triggerContext.window,\n                processContext,\n                projectedContents,\n                timestampedCollector);\n        evictorContext.evictAfter(recordsWithTimestamp, Iterables.size(recordsWithTimestamp));\n\n        // work around to fix FLINK-4369, remove the evicted elements from the windowState.\n        // this is inefficient, but there is no other way to remove elements from ListState, which\n        // is an AppendingState.\n        windowState.update(\n                recordsWithTimestamp.stream()\n                        .map(TimestampedValue::getStreamRecord)\n                        .collect(Collectors.toList()));\n    }\n\n    private void clearAllState(\n            W window, ListState<StreamRecord<IN>> windowState, MergingWindowSet<W> mergingWindows)\n            throws Exception {\n        windowState.clear();\n        triggerContext.clear();\n        processContext.window = window;\n        processContext.clear();\n        if (mergingWindows != null) {\n            mergingWindows.retireWindow(window);\n            mergingWindows.persist();\n        }\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * {@code EvictorContext} is a utility for handling {@code Evictor} invocations. It can be\n     * reused by setting the {@code key} and {@code window} fields. No internal state must be kept\n     * in the {@code EvictorContext}.\n     */\n    class EvictorContext implements Evictor.EvictorContext {\n\n        protected K key;\n        protected W window;\n\n        public EvictorContext(K key, W window) {\n            this.key = key;\n            this.window = window;\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public long getCurrentProcessingTime() {\n            return internalTimerService.currentProcessingTime();\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public long getCurrentWatermark() {\n            return internalTimerService.currentWatermark();\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public MetricGroup getMetricGroup() {\n            return EvictingWindowOperator.this.getMetricGroup();\n        }\n\n        public K getKey() {\n            return key;\n        }\n\n        void evictBefore(Iterable<TimestampedValue<IN>> elements, int size) {\n            evictor.evictBefore((Iterable) elements, size, window, this);\n        }\n\n        void evictAfter(Iterable<TimestampedValue<IN>> elements, int size) {\n            evictor.evictAfter((Iterable) elements, size, window, this);\n        }\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void open() throws Exception {\n        super.open();\n\n        evictorContext = new EvictorContext(null, null);\n        evictingWindowState =\n                (InternalListState<K, W, StreamRecord<IN>>)\n                        getOrCreateKeyedState(windowSerializer, evictingWindowStateDescriptor);\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void close() throws Exception {\n        super.close();\n        evictorContext = null;\n    }\n\n    // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\n    // Getters for testing\n    // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    @VisibleForTesting\n    public Evictor<? super IN, ? super W> getEvictor() {\n        return evictor;\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    @VisibleForTesting\n    @SuppressWarnings(\"unchecked, rawtypes\")\n    public StateDescriptor<? extends AppendingState<IN, Iterable<IN>>, ?> getStateDescriptor() {\n        return (StateDescriptor<? extends AppendingState<IN, Iterable<IN>>, ?>)\n                evictingWindowStateDescriptor;\n    }\n}\n",
  "replaceAll": false,
  "structuredPatch": [

        "+                                                                + mergeResult);",
        "+                                            } else if (!windowAssigner.isEventTime()",
        "+                                                    && mergeResult.maxTimestamp()",
        "+                                                            <= internalTimerService",
        "+                                                                    .currentProcessingTime()) {",
        "+                                                throw new UnsupportedOperationException(",
        "+                                                        \"The end timestamp of a \"",
        "+                                                                + \"processing-time window cannot become earlier than the current processing time \"",
        "+                                                                + \"by merging. Current processing time: \"",
        "+                                                                + internalTimerService",
        "+                                                                        .currentProcessingTime()",
        "+                                                                + \" window: \"",
        "+                                                                + mergeResult);",
        "+                                            }",
        " ",
        "-                                        triggerContext.key = key;",
        "-                                        triggerContext.window = mergeResult;",
        "+                                            triggerContext.key = key;",
        "+                                            triggerContext.window = mergeResult;",
        " ",
        "-                                        triggerContext.onMerge(mergedWindows);",
        "+                                            triggerContext.onMerge(mergedWindows);",
        " ",
        "-                                        for (W m : mergedWindows) {",
        "-                                            triggerContext.window = m;",
        "-                                            triggerContext.clear();",
        "-                                            deleteCleanupTimer(m);",
        "+                                            for (W m : mergedWindows) {",
        "+                                                triggerContext.window = m;",
        "+                                                triggerContext.clear();",
        "+                                                deleteCleanupTimer(m);",
        "+                                            }",
        "+",
        "+                                            // merge the merged state windows into the newly resulting",
        "+                                            // state window",
        "+                                            evictingWindowState.mergeNamespaces(",
        "+                                                    stateWindowResult, mergedStateWindows);",
        "                                         }",
        "+                                    });",
        "+                } catch (UnsupportedOperationException e) {",
        "+                    // If a late element would result in merging into an already-expired window,",
        "+                    // skip it and treat it as a late element to be side-output later",
        "+                    continue;",
        "+                }",
        " ",
        "-                                        // merge the merged state windows into the newly resulting",
        "-                                        // state window",
        "-                                        evictingWindowState.mergeNamespaces(",
        "-                                                stateWindowResult, mergedStateWindows);",
        "-                                    }",
        "-                                });",
        "-",
        "                 // drop if the window is already late",
        "                 if (isWindowLate(actualWindow)) {",
        "                     mergingWindows.retireWindow(actualWindow);"
      ],
      "newLines": 69,
      "newStart": 128,
      "oldLines": 62,
      "oldStart": 128
    }
  ],
  "userModified": false
}

Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/flink-streaming-java/src/test/java/org/apache/flink/streaming/runtime/operators/windowing/WindowOperatorTest.java",
  "new_string": "    @ParameterizedTest(name = \"Enable async state = {0}\")",
  "old_string": "    @Test\n    void testLateDataWithMergingSessionWindowsShouldBeSideOutput() throws Exception {\n        // Test: Verifies that when a late element would be assigned to a merging window\n        // that is already expired, the element is side-output as late data.\n        // This tests the specific bug fix for late data handling in merging windows.\n        final int gapSize = 3;\n        final long lateness = 0;\n\n        ReducingStateDescriptor<Tuple2<String, Integer>> stateDesc =\n                new ReducingStateDescriptor<>(\n                        \"window-contents\",\n                        new SumReducer(),\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE.createSerializer(new SerializerConfigImpl()));\n\n        WindowOperatorFactory<\n                        String,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Tuple3<String, Long, Long>,\n                        TimeWindow>\n                operator =\n                        new WindowOperatorFactory<>(\n                                EventTimeSessionWindows.withGap(Duration.ofSeconds(gapSize)),\n                                new TimeWindow.Serializer(),\n                                new TupleKeySelector(),\n                                BasicTypeInfo.STRING_TYPE_INFO.createSerializer(\n                                        new SerializerConfigImpl()),\n                                stateDesc,\n                                new InternalSingleValueWindowFunction<>(\n                                        new ReducedSessionWindowFunction()),\n                                PurgingTrigger.of(EventTimeTrigger.create()),\n                                lateness,\n                                lateOutputTag);\n\n        OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple3<String, Long, Long>>\n                testHarness = createTestHarness(operator);\n\n        testHarness.open();\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> expected = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> sideExpected = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();\n\n        // Element 1: timestamp=1000, creates window [1000, 1003]\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 1000));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(1500));\n\n        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key2-1\", 1000L, 1003L), 1002));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(1500));\n\n        // Element 2 at timestamp 1001 (LATE: 1001 + 0 <= 1500 is TRUE)\n        // Even though Element 1 has been processed, a late Element 2 should be side-output\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 1001));\n        sideExpected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 1001));\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> actual = testHarness.getOutput();\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<StreamRecord<Tuple2<String, Integer>>> sideActual =\n                testHarness.getSideOutput(lateOutputTag);\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\", expected, actual, new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"SideOutput was not correct.\",\n                sideExpected,\n                (Iterable) sideActual,\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n\n        testHarness.close();\n    }\n\n    @ParameterizedTest(name = \"Enable async state = {0}\")",
  "replace_all": false
}

window\n        // that is already expired, the element is side-output as late data.\n        // This tests the specific bug fix for late data handling in merging windows.\n        final int gapSize = 3;\n        final long lateness = 0;\n\n        ReducingStateDescriptor<Tuple2<String, Integer>> stateDesc =\n                new ReducingStateDescriptor<>(\n                        \"window-contents\",\n                        new SumReducer(),\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE.createSerializer(new SerializerConfigImpl()));\n\n        WindowOperatorFactory<\n                        String,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Tuple3<String, Long, Long>,\n                        TimeWindow>\n                operator =\n                        new WindowOperatorFactory<>(\n                                EventTimeSessionWindows.withGap(Duration.ofSeconds(gapSize)),\n                                new TimeWindow.Serializer(),\n                                new TupleKeySelector(),\n                                BasicTypeInfo.STRING_TYPE_INFO.createSerializer(\n        lateOutputTag);\n\n        OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple3<String, Long, Long>>\n                testHarness = createTestHarness(operator);\n\n        testHarness.open();\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> expected = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> sideExpected = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();\n\n        // Element 1: timestamp=1000, creates window [1000, 1003]\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 1000));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(1500));\n\n        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key2-1\", 1000L, 1003L), 1002));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(1500));\n\n        // Element 2 at timestamp 1001 (LATE: 1001 + 0 <= 1500 is TRUE)\n        // Even though Element 1 has been processed, a late Element 2 should be side-output\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 1001));\n        sideExpected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 1001));\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> actual = testHarness.getOutput();\n org.mockito.Mockito.mock;\nimport static org.mockito.Mockito.when;\n\n/** Tests for {@link WindowOperator}. */\nclass WindowOperatorTest {\n\n    private interface HarnessProvider<IN, OUT> {\n        OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple2<String, Integer>>\n                create();\n    }\n\n    private static final TypeInformation<Tuple2<String, Integer>> STRING_INT_TUPLE =\n            TypeInformation.of(new TypeHint<Tuple2<String, Integer>>() {});\n\n    // For counting if close() is called the correct number of times on the SumReducer\n    private static AtomicInteger closeCalled = new AtomicInteger(0);\n\n    // late arriving event OutputTag<StreamRecord<IN>>\n    private static final OutputTag<Tuple2<String, Integer>> lateOutputTag =\n            new OutputTag<Tuple2<String, Integer>>(\"late-output\") {};\n\n    private void testSlidingEventTimeWindows(\n            HarnessProvider<Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple2<String, Integer>> harnessProvider)\n            throws Exception {\n\n        OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple2<String, Integer>>\n                testHarness = harnessProvider.create();\n\n        testHarness.setup();\n        testHarness.open();\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> expectedOutput = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();\n\n        // add elements out-of-order\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 3999));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 3000));\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 20));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 0));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 999));\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 1998));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 1999));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 1000));\n\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 3), 999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(999));\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(1999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 3), 1999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 3), 1999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(1999));\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(2999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 3), 2999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 3), 2999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(2999));\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n\n        // do a snapshot, close and restore again\n        OperatorSubtaskState snapshot = testHarness.snapshot(0L, 0L);\n        testHarness.close();\n\n        expectedOutput.clear();\n        testHarness = harnessProvider.create();\n        testHarness.setup();\n        testHarness.initializeState(snapshot);\n        testHarness.open();\n\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(3999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 5), 3999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(3999));\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(4999));\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(5999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 2), 5999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(5999));\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n\n        // those don't have any effect...\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(6999));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(7999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(6999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(7999));\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n\n        testHarness.close();\n    }\n\n    @SuppressWarnings(\"unchecked\")\n    @ParameterizedTest(name = \"Enable async state = {0}\")\n    @ValueSource(booleans = {false, true})\n    void testSlidingEventTimeWindowsReduce(boolean enableAsyncState) throws Exception {\n        closeCalled.set(0);\n\n        final int windowSize = 3;\n        final int windowSlide = 1;\n\n        ReducingStateDescriptor<Tuple2<String, Integer>> stateDesc =\n                new ReducingStateDescriptor<>(\n                        \"window-contents\",\n                        new SumReducer(),\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE.createSerializer(new SerializerConfigImpl()));\n\n        WindowOperatorFactory<\n                        String,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        TimeWindow>\n                operator =\n                  Duration.ofSeconds(windowSlide)),\n                                new TimeWindow.Serializer(),\n                                new TupleKeySelector(),\n new InternalSingleValueWindowFunction<>(\n                                        new PassThroughWindowFunction<\n                                                String, TimeWindow, Tuple2<String, Integer>>()),\n                                EventTimeTrigger.create(),\n                                0,\n                                null /* late data output tag */);\n\n        WindowOperatorBuilder<Tuple2<String, Integer>, String, TimeWindow> builder =\n                new WindowOperatorBuilder<>(\n                        SlidingEventTimeWindows.of(\n STRING_INT_TUPLE,\n                        new TupleKeySelector(),\n                        TypeInformation.of(String.class));\n\n        testSlidingEventTimeWindows(\n                enableAsyncState\n                        ? () ->\n                                createAsyncTestHarness(\n                                        builder.asyncReduce(\n                                                new SumReducer(),\n                                                new PassThroughWindowFunction<>()))\n                        : () -> createTestHarness(operator));\n    }\n\n    @ParameterizedTest(name = \"Enable async state = {0}\")\n    @ValueSource(booleans = {false, true})\n    void testSlidingEventTimeWindowsApply(boolean enableAsyncState) throws Exception {\n        closeCalled.set(0);\n\n        final int =\n                        new WindowOperatorFactory<>(\n                                SlidingEventTimeWindows.of(\n                                        Duration.ofSeconds(windowSize),\n  EventTimeTrigger.create(),\n                        new ExecutionConfig(),\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE,\n                        new TupleKeySelector(),\n                        TypeInformation.of(String.class));\n\n        testSlidingEventTimeWindows(\n                enableAsyncState\n                        ? () -> createAsyncTestHarness(builder.asyncApply(new RichSumReducer<>()))\n                        : () -> createTestHarness(operator));\n\n        // we close once in the rest...\n        assertThat(closeCalled).as(\"Close was not called.\").hasValue(2);\n    }\n\n    private void testTumblingEventTimeWindows(\n            HarnessProvider<Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple2<String, Integer>> harnessProvider)\n            throws Exception {\n        OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple2<String, Integer>>\n                testHarness = harnessProvider.create();\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> expectedOutput = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();\n\n        testHarness.open();\n\n        // add elements out-of-order\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 3999));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 3000));\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 20));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 0));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 999));\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 1998));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 1999));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 1000));\n\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(999));\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(1999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(1999));\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n\n        // do a snapshot, close and restore again\n        OperatorSubtaskState snapshot = testHarness.snapshot(0L, 0L);\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n        testHarness.close();\n\n        testHarness = harnessProvider.create();\n        expectedOutput.clear();\n        testHarness.setup();\n        testHarness.initializeState(snapshot);\n        testHarness.open();\n\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(2999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 3), 2999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 3), 2999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(2999));\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(3999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(3999));\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(4999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(4999));\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(5999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 2), 5999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(5999));\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n\n        // those don't have any effect...\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(6999));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(7999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(6999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(7999));\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n\n        testHarness.close();\n    }\n\n    @ParameterizedTest(name = \"Enable async state = {0}\")\n    @ValueSource(booleans = {false, true})\n    @SuppressWarnings(\"unchecked\")\n    void testTumblingEventTimeWindowsReduce(boolean enableAsyncState) throws Exception {\n        closeCalled.set(0);\n\n        final int windowSize = 3;\n\n        ReducingStateDescriptor<Tuple2<String, Integer>> stateDesc =\n                new ReducingStateDescriptor<>(\n                        \"window-contents\",\n                        new SumReducer(),\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE.createSerializer(new SerializerConfigImpl()));\n\n        WindowOperatorFactory<\n                        String,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        TimeWindow>\n                operator =\n                        new WindowOperatorFactory<>(\n                                TumblingEventTimeWindows.of(Duration.ofSeconds(windowSize)),\n                                new TimeWindow.Serializer(),\n                                new TupleKeySelector(),\n                                BasicTypeInfo.STRING_TYPE_INFO.createSerializer(\n                                        new SerializerConfigImpl()),\n                                stateDesc,\n                                new InternalSingleValueWindowFunction<>(\n                                        new PassThroughWindowFunction<\n                                                String, TimeWindow, Tuple2<String, Integer>>()),\n                                EventTimeTrigger.create(),\n                                0,\n                                null /* late data output tag */);\n\n        WindowOperatorBuilder<Tuple2<String, Integer>, String, TimeWindow> builder =\n                new WindowOperatorBuilder<>(\n                        TumblingEventTimeWindows.of(Duration.ofSeconds(windowSize)),\n                        EventTimeTrigger.create(),\n                        new ExecutionConfig(),\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE,\n                        new TupleKeySelector(),\n                        TypeInformation.of(String.class));\n\n        testTumblingEventTimeWindows(\n                enableAsyncState\n                        ? () ->\n                                createAsyncTestHarness(\n                                        builder.asyncReduce(\n                                                new SumReducer(),\n                                                new PassThroughWindowFunction<>()))\n                        : () -> createTestHarness(operator));\n    }\n\n    @ParameterizedTest(name = \"Enable async state = {0}\")\n    @ValueSource(booleans = {false, true})\n    @SuppressWarnings(\"unchecked\")\n    void testTumblingEventTimeWindowsApply(boolean enableAsyncState) throws Exception {\n        closeCalled.set(0);\n\n        final int windowSize = 3;\n\n        ListStateDescriptor<Tuple2<String, Integer>> stateDesc =\n                new ListStateDescriptor<>(\n                        \"window-contents\",\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE.createSerializer(new SerializerConfigImpl()));\n\n        WindowOperatorFactory<\n                        String,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Iterable<Tuple2<String, Integer>>,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        TimeWindow>\n                operator =\n                        new WindowOperatorFactory<>(\n                                TumblingEventTimeWindows.of(Duration.ofSeconds(windowSize)),\n                                new TimeWindow.Serializer(),\n                                new TupleKeySelector(),\n                                BasicTypeInfo.STRING_TYPE_INFO.createSerializer(\n                                        new SerializerConfigImpl()),\n                                stateDesc,\n                                new InternalIterableWindowFunction<>(\n                                        new RichSumReducer<TimeWindow>()),\n                                EventTimeTrigger.create(),\n                                0,\n                                null /* late data output tag */);\n\n        WindowOperatorBuilder<Tuple2<String, Integer>, String, TimeWindow> builder =\n                new WindowOperatorBuilder<>(\n                        TumblingEventTimeWindows.of(Duration.ofSeconds(windowSize)),\n                        EventTimeTrigger.create(),\n                        new ExecutionConfig(),\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE,\n                        new TupleKeySelector(),\n                        TypeInformation.of(String.class));\n\n        testTumblingEventTimeWindows(\n                enableAsyncState\n                        ? () -> createAsyncTestHarness(builder.asyncApply(new RichSumReducer<>()))\n                        : () -> createTestHarness(operator));\n\n        // we close once in the rest...\n        assertThat(closeCalled).as(\"Close was not called.\").hasValue(2);\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    @SuppressWarnings(\"unchecked\")\n    void testSessionWindows() throws Exception {\n        closeCalled.set(0);\n\n        final int sessionSize = 3;\n\n        ListStateDescriptor<Tuple2<String, Integer>> stateDesc =\n                new ListStateDescriptor<>(\n                        \"window-contents\",\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE.createSerializer(new SerializerConfigImpl()));\n\n        WindowOperatorFactory<\n                        String,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Iterable<Tuple2<String, Integer>>,\n                        Tuple3<String, Long, Long>,\n                        TimeWindow>\n                operator =\n                        new WindowOperatorFactory<>(\n                                EventTimeSessionWindows.withGap(Duration.ofSeconds(sessionSize)),\n                                new TimeWindow.Serializer(),\n                                new TupleKeySelector(),\n                                BasicTypeInfo.STRING_TYPE_INFO.createSerializer(\n                                        new SerializerConfigImpl()),\n                                stateDesc,\n                                new InternalIterableWindowFunction<>(new SessionWindowFunction()),\n                                EventTimeTrigger.create(),\n                                0,\n                                null /* late data output tag */);\n\n        OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple3<String, Long, Long>>\n                testHarness = createTestHarness(operator);\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> expectedOutput = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();\n\n        testHarness.open();\n\n        // add elements out-of-order\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 0));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 2), 1000));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 3), 2500));\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 10));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 2), 1000));\n\n        // do a snapshot, close and restore again\n        OperatorSubtaskState snapshot = testHarness.snapshot(0L, 0L);\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple3ResultSortComparator());\n        testHarness.close();\n\n        testHarness = createTestHarness(operator);\n        testHarness.setup();\n        testHarness.initializeState(snapshot);\n        testHarness.open();\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 3), 2500));\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 4), 5501));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 5), 6000));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 5), 6000));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 6), 6050));\n\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(12000));\n\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key1-6\", 10L, 5500L), 5499));\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key2-6\", 0L, 5500L), 5499));\n\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key2-20\", 5501L, 9050L), 9049));\n        expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(12000));\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 10), 15000));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 20), 15000));\n\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(17999));\n\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key2-30\", 15000L, 18000L), 17999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(17999));\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple3ResultSortComparator());\n\n        testHarness.close();\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    @SuppressWarnings(\"unchecked\")\n    void testSessionWindowsWithProcessFunction() throws Exception {\n        closeCalled.set(0);\n\n        final int sessionSize = 3;\n\n        ListStateDescriptor<Tuple2<String, Integer>> stateDesc =\n                new ListStateDescriptor<>(\n 0));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 2), 1000));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 3), 2500));\n\n createTestHarness(operator);\n        testHarness.setup();\n        testHarness.initializeState(snapshot);\n        testHarness.open();\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 3), 2500));\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 4), 5501));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 5), 6000));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 5), 6000));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 6), 6050));\n\n testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 10), 15000));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 20), 15000));\n\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(17999));\n\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key2-30\", 15000L, 18000L), 17999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(17999));\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple3ResultSortComparator());\n\n        testHarness.close();\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    @SuppressWarnings(\"unchecked\")\n    void testReduceSessionWindows() throws Exception {\n        closeCalled.set(0);\n\n        final int sessionSize = 3;\n\n        ReducingStateDescriptor<Tuple2<String, Integer>> stateDesc =\n                new ReducingStateDescriptor<>(\n                        \"window-contents\",\n                        new SumReducer(),\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE.createSerializer(new SerializerConfigImpl()));\n\n        WindowOperatorFactory<\n                        String,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Tuple3<String, Long, Long>,\n                          new TimeWindow.Serializer(),\n                                new TupleKeySelector(),\n                                BasicTypeInfo.STRING_TYPE_INFO.createSerializer(\n                     new ReducedSessionWindowFunction()),\n                                EventTimeTrigger.create(),\n                                0,\n                                null /* late data output tag */);\n\n        OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple3<String, Long, Long>>\n                testHarness = createTestHarness(operator);\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> expectedOutput = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();\n\n        testHarness.open();\n\n        // add elements out-of-order\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 0));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 2), 1000));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 3), 2500));\n\n        // do a snapshot, close and restore again\n        OperatorSubtaskState snapshot = testHarness.snapshot(0L, 0L);\n        testHarness.close();\n\n        testHarness = createTestHarness(operator);\n        testHarness.setup();\n        testHarness.initializeState(snapshot);\n        testHarness.open();\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 10));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 2), 1000));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 3), 2500));\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 4), 5501));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 5), 6000));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 5), 6000));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 6), 6050));\n\n Tuple3<>(\"key2-6\", 0L, 5500L), 5499));\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key2-20\", 5501L, 9050L), 9049));\n        expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(12000));\n\n Watermark(17999));\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple3ResultSortComparator());\n\n        testHarness.close();\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    @SuppressWarnings(\"unchecked\")\n    void testReduceSessionWindowsWithProcessFunction() throws                        \"window-contents\",\n                        new SumReducer(),\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE.createSerializer(new SerializerConfigImpl()));\n\n Long, Long>,\n                        TimeWindow>\n                operator =\n                        new WindowOperatorFactory<>(\n                                EventTimeSessionWindows.withGap(Duration.ofSeconds(sessionSize)),\n                                new TimeWindow.Serializer(),\n                                new TupleKeySelector(),\n                                BasicTypeInfo.STRING_TYPE_INFO.createSerializer(\n                                        new SerializerConfigImpl()),\n                                stateDesc,\n EventTimeTrigger.create(),\n                                0,\n                                null /* late data output tag */);\n\n        OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<Tuple2<String, Integer>, testHarness.open();\n\n        // add elements out-of-order\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 0));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 2), 1000));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 3), 2500));\n\n        // do a snapshot, close and restore again\n testHarness.initializeState(snapshot);\n        testHarness.open();\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 10));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 2), 1000));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 3), 2500));\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 4), 5501));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 5), 6000));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 5), 6000));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 6), 6050));\n\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(12000));\n\n        expectedOutput.add(new 15000));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 20), 15000));\n\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(17999));\n\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key2-30\", 15000L, 18000L), 17999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(17999));\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple3ResultSortComparator());\n\n        testHarness.close();\n    }\n\n    /** This tests whether merging works correctly with the CountTrigger. */\n    @Test\n    void testSessionWindowsWithCountTrigger() throws Exception {\n        closeCalled.set(0);\n\n        final int sessionSize = 3;\n\n        ListStateDescriptor<Tuple2<String, Integer>> stateDesc =\n                new ListStateDescriptor<>(\n                        \"window-contents\",\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE.createSerializer(new SerializerConfigImpl()));\n\n        WindowOperatorFactory<\n                        String,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Iterable<Tuple2<String, Integer>>,\n                        Tuple3<String, Long, Long>,\n                        TimeWindow>\n                operator =\n                        new WindowOperatorFactory<>(\n                                EventTimeSessionWindows.withGap(Duration.ofSeconds(sessionSize)),\n                                new TimeWindow.Serializer(),\n                                new TupleKeySelector(),\n                                BasicTypeInfo.STRING_TYPE_INFO.createSerializer(\n                                        new SerializerConfigImpl()),\n                                stateDesc,\n                                new InternalIterableWindowFunction<>(new SessionWindowFunction()),\n                                PurgingTrigger.of(CountTrigger.of(4)),\n                                0,\n                                null /* late data output tag */);\n\n        OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple3<String, Long, Long>>\n                testHarness = createTestHarness(operator);\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> expectedOutput = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();\n\n        testHarness.open();\n\n        // add elements out-of-order\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 0));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 2), 1000));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 3), 2500));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 4), 3500));\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 10));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 2), 1000));\n\n        // do a snapshot, close and restore again\n        OperatorSubtaskState snapshot = testHarness.snapshot(0L, 0L);\n        testHarness.close();\n\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key2-10\", 0L, 6500L), 6499));\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple3ResultSortComparator());\n        expectedOutput.clear();\n\n        testHarness = createTestHarness(operator);\n        testHarness.setup();\n        testHarness.initializeState(snapshot);\n        testHarness.open();\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 3), 2500));\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 6000));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 2), 6500));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 3), 7000));\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple3ResultSortComparator());\n\n        // add an element that merges the two \"key1\" sessions, they should now have count 6, and\n        // therefore fire\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 10), 4500));\n\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key1-22\", 10L, 10000L), 9999L));\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple3ResultSortComparator());\n\n        testHarness.close();\n    }\n\n    /** This tests whether merging works correctly with the ContinuousEventTimeTrigger. */\n    @Test\n    void testSessionWindowsWithContinuousEventTimeTrigger() throws Exception {\n        closeCalled.set(0);\n\n        final int sessionSize = 3;\n\n        ListStateDescriptor<Tuple2<String, Integer>> stateDesc =\n                new ListStateDescriptor<>(\n                        \"window-contents\",\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE.createSerializer(new SerializerConfigImpl()));\n\n        WindowOperatorFactory<\n                        String,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Iterable<Tuple2<String, Integer>>,\n                        Tuple3<String, Long, Long>,\n                        TimeWindow>\n                operator =\n                        new WindowOperatorFactory<>(\n                                EventTimeSessionWindows.withGap(Duration.ofSeconds(sessionSize)),\n                                new TimeWindow.Serializer(),\n                                new TupleKeySelector(),\n                                BasicTypeInfo.STRING_TYPE_INFO.createSerializer(\n                                        new SerializerConfigImpl()),\n                                stateDesc,\n                                new InternalIterableWindowFunction<>(new SessionWindowFunction()),\n                                ContinuousEventTimeTrigger.of(Duration.ofSeconds(2)),\n                                0,\n                                null /* late data output tag */);\n\n        OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple3<String, Long, Long>>\n                testHarness = createTestHarness(operator);\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> expectedOutput = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();\n\n        testHarness.open();\n\n        // add elements out-of-order and first trigger time is 2000\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 1500));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 0));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 3), 2500));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 2), 1000));\n\n        // triggers emit and next trigger time is 4000\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(2500));\n\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key1-1\", 1500L, 4500L), 4499));\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key2-6\", 0L, 5500L), 5499));\n        expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(2500));\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 5), 4000));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(3000));\n        expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(3000));\n\n        // do a snapshot, close and restore again\n        OperatorSubtaskState snapshot = testHarness.snapshot(0L, 0L);\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n
